hypothesis: If tensor completion methods are used as surrogate models for predicting mechanical properties of lattice structures, then they will achieve superior predictive accuracy (higher R² scores) compared to conventional machine learning baselines (Gaussian Process, XGBoost, MLP) under both uniform and biased sampling conditions, because tensor completion exploits the inherent multi-dimensional structure of the lattice design parameter space to enable more efficient generalization from sparse and non-uniformly distributed training data.
